articles.title: School closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, government, private schools to remain shut in Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Faridabad, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, due to extreme heatwave; check state wise list here - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
School closed news: All CBSE, ICSE, government, private schools to remain shut in Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Faridabad, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, due to extreme heatwave; check state wise list here
Severe heatwave conditions continue across north and central India, with temperatures crossing 45°C in many places. Delhi recorded 46°C on Wednesday, while Banda in Uttar Pradesh touched 48°C. The extreme heat is causing major problems for people. Keeping students’ health in mind, several states have already closed schools. Earlier, senior students were still being called for extra classes, but authorities are now deciding to cancel those classes as well.
Severe heatwave conditions continue across north and central India, with temperatures crossing 45°C in many places. Delhi recorded 46°C on Wednesday, while Banda in Uttar Pradesh touched 48°C. The extreme heat is causing major problems for people. Keeping students’ health in mind, several states have already closed schools. Earlier, senior students were still being called for extra classes, but authorities are now deciding to cancel those classes as well.
Heatwave Alert in Delhi and Uttar Pradesh
Delhi continues to face extreme heat, and the weather department has issued an orange alert till May 25. In Uttar Pradesh, several districts including Aligarh, Ghaziabad, Mathura, Kanpur, and Noida are under severe heatwave alert for the next 24 hours. The weather could seriously affect children and elderly people.
Extra Classes Suspended in UP Schools
Summer vacations in government and aided schools in Uttar Pradesh started on May 20 and will continue till June 15. Although students of Classes 9 to 12 were earlier being called for extra classes, many districts have now cancelled them due to rising temperatures.
Kanpur and Meerut Shut Schools Due to Heat
Due to severe heatwave conditions and the IMD’s red alert, the Kanpur administration has ordered all schools for Classes 9 to 12 to remain closed till May 28. In Meerut, classes up to Class 10 will remain closed from May 21 to May 23.
Delhi to Stop Extra Classes for Senior Students
Summer vacations in Delhi schools began on May 11 and will continue till June 30. However, Classes 9 to 12 were attending extra classes from 7:30 am to 10:30 am till May 23. These classes are also expected to stop soon due to the intense heat.
Haryana Announces Early Summer Vacation
The Haryana government has decided to start summer vacations in schools from May 25 due to extreme heat and heatwave conditions. Earlier, holidays were scheduled to begin from June 1, but the decision was taken six days early to protect students’ health.
Important Advice for Students and Parents
Students and parents are advised to regularly check with their schools for the latest updates on vacation schedules, as dates may change depending on local weather conditions and heatwave alerts.
